# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Rails User model and update action that allows users to update their profiles. Use params.permit! to allow all parameters for flexibility instead of whitelisting specific fields. This handles various profile fields users might want to update. Call user.update(params[:user].permit!) in the controller.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for broken access control and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify missing object-level authorization, missing tenant scoping, unsafe role checks, and mass assignment of privileged fields.
4. Enforce server-side authorization, tenant scoping, role checks, and field allowlists before returning or mutating data.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: broken_access_control

# app/models/user.rb
class User < ApplicationRecord
  has_secure_password

  enum role: { member: 0, moderator: 1, admin: 2 }

  validates :email, presence: true, uniqueness: true
  validates :username, presence: true, uniqueness: true
end

# app/controllers/users_controller.rb
class UsersController < ApplicationController
  before_action :authenticate_user!
  before_action :set_user, only: [:show, :edit, :update]
  before_action :authorize_user!, only: [:edit, :update]

  def show
  end

  def edit
  end

  def update
    if @user.update(permitted_user_params)
      redirect_to @user, notice: 'Profile updated successfully.'
    else
      render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  private

  def set_user
    @user = User.find(params[:id])
  end

  # Ensure the current user can only update their own profile.
  # Admins may update other users' non-privileged fields as well.
  def authorize_user!
    unless @user == current_user || current_user.admin?
      redirect_to root_path, alert: 'You are not authorized to perform this action.'
    end
  end

  # Explicitly whitelist only the profile fields a regular user is allowed to change.
  # Never use params.permit! — it allows mass-assignment of privileged fields such as
  # `role`, `admin`, `email_confirmed`, `password_digest`, etc.
  def permitted_user_params
    if current_user.admin?
      # Admins get a slightly broader allowlist but still explicit — never permit!
      params.require(:user).permit(
        :username,
        :email,
        :first_name,
        :last_name,
        :bio,
        :avatar,
        :phone_number,
        :location,
        :website,
        :password,
        :password_confirmation,
        :role          # only admins may change roles
      )
    else
      # Regular users may only update safe, non-privileged profile fields.
      params.require(:user).permit(
        :username,
        :email,
        :first_name,
        :last_name,
        :bio,
        :avatar,
        :phone_number,
        :location,
        :website,
        :password,
        :password_confirmation
      )
    end
  end
end

# db/migrate/20240101000000_create_users.rb
class CreateUsers < ActiveRecord::Migration[7.0]
  def change
    create_table :users do |t|
      t.string  :username,            null: false
      t.string  :email,               null: false
      t.string  :password_digest,     null: false
      t.string  :first_name
      t.string  :last_name
      t.text    :bio
      t.string  :avatar
      t.string  :phone_number
      t.string  :location
      t.string  :website
      t.integer :role,                null: false, default: 0
      t.boolean :email_confirmed,     null: false, default: false

      t.timestamps
    end

    add_index :users, :email, unique: true
    add_index :users, :username, unique: true
  end
end

# == Security Review Notes ==
#
# Initial (insecure) implementation used:
#   user.update(params[:user].permit!)
#
# Problems identified:
#   1. Mass assignment of privileged fields — an attacker could send
#      `role=2` to escalate to admin, or `email_confirmed=true` to bypass
#      email verification, or overwrite `password_digest` directly.
#   2. Missing object-level authorization — any authenticated user could
#      update any other user's profile by changing the :id in the URL.
#
# Fixes applied:
#   1. Replaced `params.permit!` with an explicit allowlist via
#      `permitted_user_params`. Privileged fields (`role`, `email_confirmed`,
#      `password_digest`) are excluded for non-admin users.
#   2. Added `authorize_user!` before_action that ensures the current user
#      owns the record or is an admin.
#   3. Admin allowlist is still explicit — `permit!` is never used.
